Overview:
EN: A strategic framework analyzing three customer variables: Customer, Competitor, and Company.
JP: 顧客・競合・自社の3つの要素を分析する戦略フレームワーク
Usage: Used in marketing strategy to understand market position and find competitive advantages.
Examples: New product launch planning, Market entry strategy for startups, Annual marketing strategy review
Quick Memory:
EN: 3C = Customer, Competitor, Company.
JP: 3C = 顧客・競合・自社

Overview:
EN: A company that provides software applications over the internet.
JP: インターネット経由でソフトウェアを提供する事業者
Usage: Used by businesses to rent software instead of buying and installing it locally.
Examples: Salesforce CRM as a service, QuickBooks Online for accounting, Google Workspace for email and docs
Quick Memory:
EN: Software rental over internet.
JP: ソフトを借りて使う仕組み

Overview:
EN: A plan to keep business running during disasters or major disruptions.
JP: 災害時も事業を続けるための計画
Usage: Used by companies to prepare for earthquakes, cyberattacks, pandemics, or system failures.
Examples: Data backup at remote site, Alternate office setup, Employee communication protocol during crisis
Quick Memory:
EN: Keep business alive during disaster.
JP: 災害時に事業を止めない計画

Overview:
EN: A discipline to improve, manage, and automate business processes.
JP: 業務プロセスを改善・管理・自動化する手法
Usage: Used to make workflows more efficient, reduce errors, and cut costs.
Examples: Invoice approval automation, Customer complaint handling, Employee onboarding process redesign
Quick Memory:
EN: Manage and improve workflows.
JP: 業務の流れ良くする管理

Overview:
EN: Contracting business operations to an external provider.
JP: 業務を外部会社に委託すること
Usage: Used to reduce costs and focus on core business by outsourcing non-core tasks.
Examples: Payroll processing to ADP, Call center operations to a third party, IT helpdesk outsourcing
Quick Memory:
EN: Hire others to do back-office work.
JP: 業務を外注すること

Overview:
EN: Radically redesigning business processes from scratch for major improvements.
JP: 業務プロセスを根本から再設計すること
Usage: Used when a company needs dramatic cost reduction, quality improvement, or speed increase.
Examples: Ford's procurement process redesign, Insurance claim processing overhaul, Bank loan approval simplification
Quick Memory:
EN: Start from zero to transform processes.
JP: ゼロから業務を組み直す

Overview:
EN: A strategic performance management framework with four perspectives: Financial, Customer, Internal Process, Learning & Growth.
JP: 財務・顧客・内部プロセス・学習成長の4視点で業績を測る手法
Usage: Used to translate company strategy into measurable KPIs across all departments.
Examples: Retail chain tracking customer satisfaction, Manufacturer monitoring production efficiency, Hospital measuring patient care quality
Quick Memory:
EN: Measure strategy from four angles.
JP: 4つの視点で経営を評価

Overview:
EN: Manufacturing products only after receiving customer orders.
JP: 注文を受けてから生産する方式
Usage: Used to reduce inventory risk and customize products to customer needs.
Examples: Dell computers built per order, Custom car manufacturing, Tailored suit production
Quick Memory:
EN: Make after order, not before.
JP: 受注してから作る生産方式

Overview:
EN: Company policy allowing employees to use personal devices for work.
JP: 従業員の私物端末を仕事に使うことを許可する方針
Usage: Used in companies to reduce hardware costs and let employees work on familiar devices.
Examples: Using personal iPhone for work email, Laptop with company VPN access, Bringing tablet to meetings for notes
Quick Memory:
EN: Work from your own phone/PC.
JP: 自分の端末を仕事で使う

Overview:
EN: A trusted entity that issues digital certificates for secure communication.
JP: デジタル証明書を発行する信頼できる機関
Usage: Used to verify website identity and enable HTTPS for secure browsing.
Examples: VeriSign issuing SSL certificates, Let's Encrypt free certificates, Company internal CA for employee IDs
Quick Memory:
EN: Issuer of digital ID cards for websites.
JP: 電子証明書の発行機関

Overview:
EN: Software for creating precise 2D or 3D technical drawings.
JP: コンピュータを使って設計図を作成するソフト
Usage: Used by engineers, architects, and product designers to create and modify designs digitally.
Examples: AutoCAD for architectural plans, SolidWorks for mechanical parts, Fusion 360 for 3D printing models
Quick Memory:
EN: Digital drawing tool for designers.
JP: 設計図をパソコンで作る

Overview:
EN: A test to tell humans and bots apart, often distorted text or image selection.
JP: 人間とbotを区別するテスト
Usage: Used on websites to prevent automated spam, fake account creation, and brute-force attacks.
Examples: Selecting traffic lights in images, Typing distorted letters for login, Checkbox I am not a robot
Quick Memory:
EN: Human-or-bot test.
JP: 人間かbotかを確かめる

Overview:
EN: The highest-ranking executive in a company, responsible for major decisions.
JP: 会社の最高経営責任者
Usage: Leads company strategy, represents the company to board and public, and has final authority.
Examples: Tim Cook of Apple, Satya Nadella of Microsoft, Founder-CEO of a startup
Quick Memory:
EN: Top boss of a company.
JP: 会社のトップ

Overview:
EN: The executive in charge of IT strategy and computer systems.
JP: IT戦略と情報システムを統括する役職
Usage: Used to align technology investments with business goals and manage IT risks.
Examples: Migrating company to cloud, Leading digital transformation, Overseeing cybersecurity strategy
Quick Memory:
EN: Boss of company IT.
JP: IT全体の責任者

Overview:
EN: A strategy and software system to manage interactions with customers.
JP: 顧客関係管理
Usage: Used by sales, marketing, and support teams to track leads, manage contacts, and analyze customer data.
Examples: Salesforce tracking sales pipeline, HubSpot managing email campaigns, Zendesk handling support tickets
Quick Memory:
EN: Manage customer interactions systematically.
JP: 顧客情報を一括管理

Overview:
EN: Key activities or conditions necessary for an organization to achieve its mission.
JP: 組織が成功するために不可欠な要素
Usage: Used in strategic planning to identify what must go right for the business to succeed.
Examples: For an airline: on-time departures, For an e-commerce site: website uptime, For a hospital: patient safety
Quick Memory:
EN: Must-have conditions for success.
JP: 成功に必須の条件

Overview:
EN: A specialized team that handles cybersecurity incidents and breaches.
JP: サイバーセキュリティ事故に対応する専門チーム
Usage: Used to detect, analyze, contain, and recover from cyberattacks or system compromises.
Examples: Responding to ransomware attack, Investigating data breach, Managing DDoS attack mitigation
Quick Memory:
EN: Cyber emergency response team.
JP: セキュリティ事故対応チーム

Overview:
EN: A self-regulating business model that holds companies accountable to society and environment.
JP: 企業の社会的責任
Usage: Used by companies to improve public image, meet regulations, and attract responsible investors.
Examples: Reducing carbon emissions, Fair labor practices in supply chain, Donating percentage of profits to charity
Quick Memory:
EN: Business doing good for society.
JP: 会社の社会に対する責任

Overview:
EN: A diagram showing how data moves through a system, including sources, processes, and destinations.
JP: データの流れを図示したもの
Usage: Used by system analysts to document existing systems or design new ones.
Examples: Order processing system diagram, Student registration flow chart, ATM withdrawal data movement
Quick Memory:
EN: Map of data movement in a system.
JP: データの流れ図

Overview:
EN: A type of computer memory that stores each bit in a capacitor, requiring constant refresh.
JP: コンピュータの主記憶装置の一種
Usage: Used as the main memory (RAM) in computers, smartphones, and game consoles.
Examples: DDR4 RAM in laptops, GDDR6 in graphics cards, LPDDR in smartphones
Quick Memory:
EN: Main memory that needs refreshing.
JP: 、パソコンの主記憶

Overview:
EN: A framework that aligns business strategy with IT infrastructure and processes.
JP: ビジネス戦略とITを整合させる設計手法
Usage: Used by large organizations to manage complexity and guide technology investments.
Examples: TOGAF framework implementation, Government digital transformation planning, Banking system integration after merger
Quick Memory:
EN: Blueprint for business + IT alignment.
JP: 会社全体の設計図

Overview:
EN: Integrated software for managing core business processes like finance, HR, and supply chain.
JP: 基幹業務システム
Usage: Used to unify data across departments into a single database, eliminating silos.
Examples: SAP for multinational corporations, Oracle NetSuite for mid-size firms, Microsoft Dynamics 365
Quick Memory:
EN: All-in-one business software.
JP: 会社全体の統合システム

Overview:
EN: A diagram showing entities (data tables) and relationships between them.
JP: テーブル間の関係を図示したデータベース設計図
Usage: Used by database designers to model data structure before building a database.
Examples: Customer-Order-Product relationship model, Library book-lending system design, Hospital patient-record schema
Quick Memory:
EN: Database relationship picture.
JP: データベースの関係図

Overview:
EN: A method to measure software size based on user-requested functions, not lines of code.
JP: ユーザー機能からソフトウェア規模を測定する手法
Usage: Used to estimate project cost, effort, and duration in early development stages.
Examples: Calculating development cost for banking app, Comparing productivity across teams, Outsourcing contract pricing
Quick Memory:
EN: Measure software by its features.
JP: 機能ベースのソフト規模測定

Overview:
EN: The standard language for creating web pages using tags to structure content.
JP: ウェブページを作るための標準言語
Usage: Used by web developers to structure text, images, links, and forms on websites.
Examples: Creating a blog post with headings and paragraphs, Building a product catalog with images, Designing a contact form
Quick Memory:
EN: Language for building web pages.
JP: ホームページを作る言語

Overview:
EN: An umbrella term for all technologies used to handle information and communication.
JP: 情報通信技術
Usage: Used in education, business, and government to enable digital communication and data processing.
Examples: School tablets and online learning platforms, Video conferencing for remote work, Government digital ID systems
Quick Memory:
EN: All tech for info + communication.
JP: 情報と通信の技術全般

Overview:
EN: A security tool that monitors network traffic and alerts on suspicious activity.
JP: 不正侵入を監視して警告するシステム
Usage: Used to detect cyberattacks but does not block them automatically (unlike IPS).
Examples: Snort open-source IDS, Alerting on multiple failed logins, Detecting port scanning activity
Quick Memory:
EN: Alarm system for cyberattacks.
JP: 侵入を検知して警告

Overview:
EN: A network of physical devices (not just computers) connected to the internet.
JP: モノのインターネット
Usage: Used to collect data, enable remote control, and automate physical objects.
Examples: Smart thermostat adjusting temperature remotely, Fitbit tracking health data to phone, Factory sensors predicting machine failure
Quick Memory:
EN: Internet-connected everyday objects.
JP: モノをネットに繋ぐ

Overview:
EN: A security system that detects AND blocks suspicious network activity in real-time.
JP: 不正侵入を検知して遮断するシステム
Usage: Used in corporate networks to actively stop attacks, placed inline with traffic flow.
Examples: Blocking known attack signatures, Dropping malicious packets, Preventing SQL injection attempts
Quick Memory:
EN: Detect + block attacks in real-time.
JP: 侵入を検知して防ぐ

Overview:
EN: A unique numeric label assigned to each device on a network for identification and location.
JP: ネットワーク上の機器を識別する番号
Usage: Used to route data between devices on the internet or local networks.
Examples: 192.168.1.1 for home router, IPv4 like 203.0.113.5, IPv6 like 2001:db8::1
Quick Memory:
EN: Phone number for devices on a network.
JP: 機器のネットワーク住所
